name: tandem_repeat_alpha_satellite
description: Alpha satellite (alphoid) DNA: the 171 bp tandem repeat monomer that builds the active centromere of every human chromosome. Organised hierarchically into chromosome-specific higher-order repeats (HORs) of 2-34 monomers. AT-rich (GC ~0.36) with a short, simple monomer giving very low 5-mer Shannon entropy (1.3-1.7). The T2T-CHM13 assembly fully resolved these arrays for the first time. Mapping requires high max_occ and explicit multi-position output; reliable copy assignment generally needs HOR-specific PSV catalogs.
